meta.name: PH Single-Detached Floor Plan Rules & Preferences
meta.units: SI: metres (m), square metres (sqm), cubic metres (cum), millimetres (mm)
meta.primary_legal_source: PD 1096 — National Building Code of the Philippines (NBCP)
notes: HARD constraints are statutory minimums; a generated/validated layout MUST satisfy all that apply.
SOFT preferences are design targets derived from common PH residential practice; the generator should optimize toward these but may relax them.

Detailed yard/setback tables, road-right-of-way, and parking ratios live in the NBCP Implementing Rules & Regulations (IRR, 2004) and the Local Government Unit (LGU) zoning ordinance — these override/refine PD 1096 and must be layered in per-project. Placeholders are flagged with 'source': 'IRR/LGU — TO CONFIRM'.
BP 220 (economic/socialized housing) defines SMALLER minimums (18-22 sqm dwelling). It does NOT apply to this mid-market segment, but is recorded for future segment support.

v0.2 adds: setback_usage_policy (covered vs uncovered + firewall toggle), sizing_policy (progression + PH priority), templates (the 3 provided 2BR configs), v1_scenario (locked PoC), and refined room entries (master+ensuite, common vs ensuite bath, carport/dirty-kitchen as uncovered setback elements).

hard.measurement_basis: Taken at ground level, exclusive of courts, yards, and light wells.

lot_occupancy.note: Remaining lot area (20% inside / 10% corner) is the Total Open Space within Lot (TOSL). IRR refines this with Impervious-Surface limits per zoning. source: IRR/LGU — TO CONFIRM

refinement.note: IRR Table for R-1 (single detached) typically requires front 4.5 m, side 1.5-2.0 m, rear 2.0 m. Eaves over required windows must be >=0.75 m from side/rear property lines (Sec. 704(a)). Confirm against LGU zoning before relying on these.

hard.must_adjoin_public_space: Building must adjoin or have direct access to a public space, yard, or street on at least one side.

soft.rationale: Higher ceilings aid passive cooling in tropical climate; 2.7-3.0 m common in mid-market homes.
Code minimum stair is steep/narrow; comfortable mid-market stairs are wider with lower risers and deeper treads.

stairs.note: Not used by v1 (single-storey). Retained for multi-storey v2.

hard.requires_wastewater_disposal: Connect to municipal sewer where available; otherwise Imhoff/septic tank with subsurface absorption field.

soft.source: PH residential practice; note PD 1096 governs exit corridors separately

hard.applies_when: Room has NO artificial ventilation system.

soft.rationale: Openings on two opposite/adjacent walls enable cross-breeze; passive cooling can save ~20% energy.

hard.inner_court_connection: Inner courts must connect to a street or yard via a passageway >= 1.20 m wide, or by a door through room(s).

vent_shaft.use: Mechanism by which an interior room (e.g. interior toilet) can satisfy ventilation without an exterior window.

foundation_and_structure.note: Structural rules are not layout-shaping per se, but column-grid spacing of 3.0-4.5 m is a practical soft constraint on room module sizing. source: PH practice

principle.rule: A setback is legally OPEN space, intended for natural light, ventilation, and fire safety. Only UNCOVERED elements may occupy a mandatory setback. Covered/roofed/walled structures must sit inside the buildable footprint UNLESS a firewall abutment is declared on that segment.
principle.source: PD 1096 (open-yard intent of setbacks) + IRR/LGU — TO CONFIRM

setback_usage_policy.covered_definition: A structure is 'covered' if it has a PERMANENT roof or roofed frame, or enclosing walls. A temporary, collapsible/retractable fabric shade (e.g. a sail awning on foldable or removable supports) is NOT a permanent roof and does NOT make an element 'covered'. Note: legality hinges on the shade being genuinely temporary/non-permanent; a fixed permanent roof frame risks being treated as a structure by a strict building official. source: IRR/LGU — TO CONFIRM

eaves_clearance.applies_to: Any roof projection / eaves of a covered element.

covered_structure_front_setback.note: Covered structures (incl. covered carports) must sit behind this line; uncovered/open-air parking is EXEMPT and may use the front setback.

firewall_abutment.default_rationale: DEFAULT OFF for v1 — most portable/always-compliant, since many LGU zoning ordinances and subdivision HOAs ban firewalls. Toggle per project after confirming the subdivision permits it.

when_enabled_limits.openings_facing_neighbor: NONE — no doors, windows, or vent blocks; smoke/vent must go inward or up via chimney.
when_enabled_limits.source: Firewall % limits are IRR-level (NOT base PD 1096 Sec. 804, which governs courts). Fire-resistive wall basis: PD 1096 Sec. 704 + fire-zone chapters. IRR/LGU — TO CONFIRM

sizing_policy.progression_note: Size each room at its PREFERRED dimensions first; when the envelope is tight, relax toward the comfortable/relaxed minimum (soft.min_dimensions_m), and only down to the hard code minimum (hard.min_*) as a last resort.
sizing_policy.priority_note: When space is contested, protect higher-priority spaces at higher tiers first. PH context: public/gathering spaces (Living-Dining-Kitchen) are prioritized in size OVER private bedrooms; the master bedroom is prioritized over other bedrooms.

placement_preference.rationale: Quietest, furthest from street and carport.

adjacency.requires_ensuite_access: ensuite_bath door connects ONLY to master_bedroom

hard.note: Habitable room; treated under Sec. 806(1). Merges living + dining into one open-plan space; may be L-shaped (modeled as two cells).

soft.rationale: Combined living + dining; preferred area approximates the sum of separate living (16-27) and dining (10-16) targets.

soft.work_triangle: Sink, stove, refrigerator in triangle; counter sum 3.6-7.9 m.

placement_preference.rationale: Must sit on the rear wall so its exit door opens onto the open dirty kitchen / service area in the rear setback.

retractable_shade.note: Temporary sun/rain shade; does NOT change 'covered' status or setback eligibility.

hard.note: Open-air element in the rear (or side) setback; not an enclosed room, so Sec. 806 room minimums do not bind. Must remain uncovered (no permanent roof) to stay legal in the setback, OR use a firewall abutment if firewall policy is enabled.
hard.source: PD 1096 setback open-yard intent; IRR/LGU — TO CONFIRM

soft.rationale: Heavy/aromatic cooking kept out of the indoor kitchen; sits in the rear setback, well-ventilated, open-air.

hard.ventilation: Exterior window (>=10% floor area) OR vent shaft per Sec. 809 if interior.
Exterior window (>=10% floor area) OR vent shaft per Sec. 809 if interior.

adjacency.access: accessed from public space (hallway / living / dining), NOT through a bedroom

room_catalog.label: Bath & toilet (generic base)

hard.ventilation: Exterior window (>=10% floor area) OR vent shaft per Sec. 809 if interior.

room_catalog.note: Generic base type; specialized into ensuite_bath and common_bath for v1.

room_catalog.label: Service / laundry / wash area (open, uncovered, in setback)

hard.note: Open-air laundry/wash in the rear (or side) setback; must remain uncovered.

soft.rationale: Laundry, wash, drying; kept discreet from living zones; benefits from daylight for drying.

room_catalog.label: Maid's / helper's room

hard.note: Habitable room -> full Sec. 806(1) minimum applies.

room_catalog.note: Not in v1 program; retained for larger programs.

room_catalog.label: Lanai / veranda (outdoor)

hard.note: Outdoor/semi-outdoor; not a habitable room. If kept uncovered it may occupy a setback; if permanently roofed it must sit in the footprint (or use a firewall).

soft.rationale: Extension of living space for gatherings/al fresco; strong PH preference.

room_catalog.note: Not in v1 program; retained for future.

hard.note: Uncovered open-air parking; allowed in the side (or front) setback. A COVERED carport would NOT be setback-eligible and must sit in the footprint behind the covered-structure front setback (or use a firewall). Parking provision/ratio governed by IRR & LGU zoning. source: IRR/LGU — TO CONFIRM

placement_rule.street_access: Carport sits toward the front of the chosen side yard for direct street/driveway access.
placement_rule.note: Engine evaluates both left and right and keeps the better-scoring side.

room_catalog.label: Hallway / circulation corridor

hard.note: Min clear width 0.90 m per Sec. 1207 (single-family stair/passage); area floor keeps it a usable corridor, not a token sliver.

room_catalog.note: Private-wing circulation node: bedrooms and the common T&B open onto it; one end bridges to the public LDK (dining or living).

room_catalog.label: Foyer / entry vestibule
room_catalog.note: Optional; v1 front door opens directly into the living room.

private.description: Quiet sleeping quarters; cluster together, buffer from public/service.

service.description: Work/utility loop; kitchen-dining-service forms a working loop; keep discreet from living zones.

soft_rules: Cluster bedrooms together (sleeping quarters) on the quiet side and buffer from public and service zones for acoustic/visual privacy.
Form a kitchen <-> dining <-> living spine to support multitasking without congestion.
Front door opens directly into the living room (the arrival zone) at the front/street side.
Place the kitchen on the rear wall so its exit door opens onto the open dirty kitchen / service area in the rear setback.
Prefer the master bedroom toward the rear (quietest); standard bedroom toward the front; ensuite opens only off the master; common bath accessed from public space.
Keep the carport on the quieter exterior, opposite the bedroom cluster where possible.
Prevailing winds: NE (amihan, Oct-Mar) and SW (habagat, Apr-Sep). Orient main living/sleeping openings to catch these for cross-ventilation.
Minimize large unshaded openings on W and E facades to reduce afternoon/morning solar heat gain; favor N and S exposures for habitable rooms.
Provide roof overhangs/eaves and shading devices on sun-exposed openings.
Place service spaces (carport, storage, service area) on hot W/E facades to buffer habitable rooms from heat.
Aim for openings on at least two walls per habitable room to enable cross-ventilation.
orientation_model.note: Deferred to v2 per project decision; rules retained for when orientation scoring is enabled.

templates.description: Three user-provided 2BR topology configurations (common-configs/ PDFs). They share ONE adjacency graph; they differ only in footprint aspect ratio. Treat as STARTING POINTS the engine may vary, not fixed templates.

shared_adjacency_graph.spine_layout: Private spine (bedrooms + baths) on one side; public/service spine (living -> dining -> kitchen, front to rear) on the other. Living at front (main entry), kitchen at rear (door to dirty kitchen/service in rear setback). Carport in a side setback; dirty kitchen + laundry in the rear setback.

variants.best_for_lot: narrow/deep buildable envelope; FITS the v1 ~5x11 m envelope

v1_scenario.label: v1 proof-of-concept (locked 2026-05-24)

buildable_footprint_m.note: Carport side widened to 3 m; carport itself is uncovered in that side setback and does NOT consume footprint.

setback_elements: dirty_kitchen (uncovered, rear setback, optional retractable sail)
v1_scenario.definition_of_done: Produce several distinct, fully code-compliant 2BR layouts on this lot, each rendered as a 2D SVG and passing the validator with zero hard-rule (error) violations.
pipeline: validator as fitness (lexicographic: hard -> zoning -> preferred sizes)

validation_severity_levels.error: Violates a HARD (statutory) constraint. Layout is non-compliant and must be corrected.
validation_severity_levels.warning: Violates a refinement constraint sourced from IRR/LGU that must be confirmed per project (e.g. covered element in setback without firewall; eaves clearance; firewall length limits).
validation_severity_levels.suggestion: Falls outside a SOFT preference target; layout is legal but sub-optimal for PH living.

segment_reference_BP220.note: NOT applied to mid-market. Recorded for future economic/socialized-housing segment.
